content.text:
Big checkpoint: foundation done. 7 of 11 tasks complete (TOMLs, builtins, storage, Message + API tool plumbing, working_dir). Build clean. ~1100 LOC so far.

Before I cut into the execution loop (task #29) — quick alignment check on the approach, since it's the most consequential remaining design call:

**Loop shape**:
1. Stream completes (`Done` event arrives).
2. If the assistant message has `tool_calls`, the loop fires:
   - For each call: check safety. `auto` → execute immediately. `confirm` → render approval card and **wait for user click**.
   - Push a `Role::Tool` message per call with the result (or `"rejected by user"` if denied).
   - Re-fire `start_streaming` to send the new state back to the model.
3. Repeat until the model returns no `tool_calls`.
4. Hard cap at **8 iterations per turn** to prevent runaway loops (model keeps calling tools forever). After cap, surface a toast.

**Approval flow**: confirm-safety calls park in a `pending_tool_call: Option<(call, def)>` slot on `ChatApp`. The execution loop is paused while this is `Some`. The UI renders the approval card, click handlers populate the result and resume the loop. `streaming` stays true through the whole sequence so the user can't send a new message mid-loop.

**"Allow all in this conversation" toggle**: per-conv `auto_approved_tools: HashSet<String>` (lives on `ChatApp`, not persisted — fresh per session). Adding a tool name skips the approval card for the rest of the conv but doesn't mutate the TOML.

Sound right? If yes I'll keep going; if you want to tweak anything (cap value, persistence of the auto-approve set, anything else) say so now.

content.description: New src/tools.rs. ToolDef struct with name/description/parameters/handler/safety. Handler enum (Builtin(String) | Shell(Vec<String>)). Safety enum (Auto | Confirm). Loader walks ~/.config/hchat/tools/*.toml and returns Vec<ToolDef>. JSON-schema parameters stored as serde_json::Value (passes through to API).

content.subject: Default tool TOMLs (read/list/search/write/shell)
content.description: Ship 5 toml files installed alongside the binary (or seeded into ~/.config/hchat/tools/ on first launch if empty): read_file, list_directory, search_files, write_file, run_shell. Each with handler="builtin:..." and the right safety default.

content.subject: Builtin handlers in Rust
content.description: Implement read_file (with offset/limit, 100KB cap), list_directory (recursive opt), search_files (regex over file contents — wrap walking + matching), write_file (with overwrite vs append), run_shell (sh -c, captured stdout/stderr/exit). Each takes JSON args + working_dir, returns Result<String, String>.

content.subject: Message + API: tool_calls + role=tool
content.description: Role::Tool variant. Message gains tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>> (assistant) and tool_call_id: Option<String> (tool). ChatRequest gains tools: Option<Vec<ToolDef-as-OpenAI-shape>>. Streaming Delta picks up tool_calls deltas (incremental — assemble across chunks by index).

content.subject: Tool execution loop in app.rs
content.description: After a Done event, if the assistant message has tool_calls: for each call, check safety (auto-execute or wait for confirmation), execute the handler, push a Role::Tool message with the result, then re-fire start_streaming. Loop until response has no more tool_calls. Cap at N iterations to prevent runaway loops.

content.subject: Approval card UI for confirm-safety tools
content.description: When a tool with safety=confirm fires, render an approval card inline showing tool name + args (pretty-printed). Buttons: Approve, Approve all in this conv (sets a per-conv allowlist for this tool name), Reject. Reject feeds back a "rejected by user" string as the tool result so the model knows.

content.subject: Tool call + result rendering in message list
content.description: Render assistant tool_calls as a collapsible block under the assistant message ("Called read_file({path: '...'})"). Render Role::Tool messages as a collapsible "Tool result" block tied to the call id. Keep visual hierarchy clean — tools shouldn't dominate the chat scroll.

content.subject: working_dir per-conversation setting
content.description: New conversations.working_dir TEXT column (migration). ConversationSettings gains working_dir: Option<String>. Settings panel exposes it (text field + 'pick directory' button). Defaults to ~. Tool handlers receive this dir when resolving relative paths.

content.subject: Storage: persist tool_calls + tool messages
content.description: messages.tool_calls TEXT column (JSON-serialized Vec<ToolCall>) + messages.tool_call_id TEXT (for tool role). Migration ALTERs them in. save_messages writes both; load reconstructs them on the Message struct.

content.subject: Tests: TOML parse, builtins, approval flow, tool round-trip
content.description: Parse the 5 default TOMLs. Each builtin handler with sample args (incl. error cases — file not found, path traversal). Approval flow: rejected → tool result is "rejected by user". Storage round-trip of a Message with tool_calls + a Role::Tool message.

input.prompt:
Code review the in-progress changes on branch `phase-5a-tools` of the Rust project at `/Users/heath/code/hChat`. This is Phase 5a — native tool/function calling with a user-configurable, community-pack-able TOML format.

Architecture:
- New `src/tools.rs` module: ToolDef + Handler enum (Builtin or Shell) + Safety enum (Auto or Confirm), TOML loader walking `~/.config/hchat/tools/`, 5 builtin handlers (read_file / list_directory / search_files / write_file / run_shell), shell handler with `{{name}}` substitution, OpenAI tools-array conversion.
- 5 default `.toml` files under `src/default_tools/` embedded via `include_str!` and seeded to `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch if the dir is empty.
- `Message` gains `tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>>` and `tool_call_id: Option<String>`. New `Role::Tool` variant.
- `api.rs` accumulates streaming tool_call deltas across chunks (indexed by slot, name + id arrive once, arguments piecewise) and emits a single `StreamEvent::ToolCalls` before `Done`.
- `app.rs` execution loop: on Done with tool_calls, queue them, drain auto-safety / pre-approved ones immediately (executing the tool + pushing a `Role::Tool` message), park first confirm-safety call in `pending_approval`, render approval card with Approve / Approve all in conv / Reject buttons. After the queue drains, re-fire `start_streaming` with `tool_iterations += 1`. Hard cap at 8 iterations per turn.
- `working_dir` per-conversation setting (column on conversations + UI in settings panel) used as cwd for tool execution.
- Storage migration ALTERs in `tool_calls`, `tool_call_id`, `working_dir` columns. Should be safe for users on 0.7.0/0.8.x DBs (the same ALTER-via-pragma_table_info path that was added in the 0.8.0 fix).

What I want you to do:
1. Read the diff: `git diff main...HEAD` plus `git status` for untracked files (`src/tools.rs`, `src/default_tools/*`).
2. Read enough surrounding code to understand context, especially `src/tools.rs::run_builtin` + each builtin, `src/app.rs::kick_off_tool_execution` / `process_pending_tool_calls` / `resolve_pending_approval` / `execute_tool_call`, `src/api.rs::accumulate_tool_call` + `flush_tool_calls`, the storage migrations in `src/storage.rs::ensure_v2_columns`.
3. Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test`. Both should pass — flag any failures. Tests are in `src/tools.rs::tests` (TOML parse, builtin behavior, shell substitution) and `src/storage.rs::tests` (round-trip with tool_calls + Role::Tool).
4. Risk areas to scrutinize:
   - **Safety defaults can't drift**: write_file and run_shell MUST default to `safety = "confirm"`. There's a regression test for this; verify it would actually fail if someone flipped a default. Also: does any code path bypass the safety check?
   - **Approval card flow**: the execution loop parks in `pending_approval`, the card renders inline above the input, click handlers call `resolve_pending_approval`. Verify: (a) the user can't send a new message while a tool is parked, (b) closing the conversation / starting a new one doesn't leave a stale `pending_approval`, (c) clicking Reject feeds back a "rejected by user" tool result so the model knows.
   - **Iteration cap correctness**: `tool_iterations` resets in `send_message`. Verify it's not bypassable (e.g. could a regenerate or edit reset it improperly?). Cap is 8.
   - **`auto_approved_tools` is per-session, not per-conversation**: I told the user it'd be per-conv. The `HashSet<String>` lives on `ChatApp` and is never cleared on `new_conversation` or `load_conversation`. Verify that's actually a bug (it is) and flag for fix.
   - **Path resolution in builtins**: `resolve_path` joins relative paths to `working_dir`. Are there any path-traversal concerns I should worry about (e.g. `read_file` with `../../etc/passwd` from a working_dir set to a project root)? My take: it's a power-user tool, the user authorized the call via the approval card or by setting safety=auto, so traversal is intentional. Validate that take is reasonable.
   - **Streaming tool_call accumulator**: across-chunk reassembly in `api.rs::accumulate_tool_call`. Slot-keyed, id arrives once, arguments concatenated. Verify it handles: out-of-order chunks (some providers do this), multiple concurrent tool calls in one assistant turn, missing id (currently dropped on `flush_tool_calls`).
   - **Storage migration safety**: new columns ALTERed in via `add_column_if_missing`. Verify the `tool_calls`, `tool_call_id`, `working_dir` additions follow the same pattern as the 0.8.0 fix and won't crash a 0.6.x → 0.9.0 upgrader.
   - **`kick_off_tool_execution` placement**: called after `save_current` in `process_events` end-of-stream. Verify `save_current` actually persists the assistant's `tool_calls` field (it should — `save_messages` writes `tool_calls_json`) so a crash mid-execution doesn't lose the call list.
   - **"approved decisions during streaming" is impossible**: the approval card only appears after `Done`. Verify there's no race where a fast model + tool execution leaves an orphan `pending_approval` after `streaming = false`.
   - **`run_shell` timeout**: hardcoded 30s. Reasonable? What happens to a streaming `cargo build` that takes 60s? (truncated stdout at 50KB, killed after 30s — flag if this seems like the wrong tradeoff for a power-user tool).
5. Note clippy-acceptable but human-questionable patterns.

Be specific. For every finding, cite `file:line` and explain *why*. Keep the report under 800 words. End with: ship-ready, ship-with-followups, or needs-fixes-before-shipping.

content.text:
Confirmed: neither `new_conversation` nor `load_conversation` clears `pending_approval`, `pending_tool_calls`, `auto_approved_tools`, or `tool_iterations`. **Bug**: a parked tool from one conversation would remain visible (and resolvable) after switching to another conversation, where its resolution would push a `tool_result` into the wrong conversation's `messages`.

Also `tool_iterations` not reset on regenerate/edit: regenerate from an assistant turn that was tool iteration #6 of #8, in a fresh user-driven retry from that branch, would only allow 2 more tool calls. Minor — probably wrong, but fail-closed.

Let me check one more thing: the `tool_acc` handling for missing IDs.

Looking at `flush_tool_calls`: a slot whose `id` never arrived is `filter_map`'d out (returns None). Good — doesn't panic. But it means a partial tool call is silently dropped, which could leave the model expecting a result it'll never get. Worth flagging.

And: `streaming = false` on `Done` happens before `kick_off_tool_execution` runs (in `process_events`, `Done` sets `streaming = false`, then the `if just_finished_streaming` branch fires `kick_off_tool_execution`). If all tool calls are auto, `process_pending_tool_calls` re-fires `start_streaming` synchronously, which sets `streaming = true` again. So the input briefly thinks it's not streaming (window of one event-pump cycle) — could the user race-send during that microsecond? In egui's single-thread render loop, no: all this runs in `process_events` before the input area renders again. Safe.

But the parked-confirm path leaves `streaming = false`, the approval card visible, and the user free to type and Send. **Bug confirmed.**

Let me also verify the `unused position` — looking at the code: `position: i64` was used for ordering, now `#[allow(dead_code)]`. It's set from row 6 but never read. Minor.

Let me check what happens when the model emits invalid args JSON — `execute_tool_call` returns `format!("error: invalid arguments JSON: {e}")`. Good — that error gets pushed as the tool result so the model can self-correct.

I have enough. Writing the report.

# Phase 5a Code Review — `phase-5a-tools`

**Build/test status:** `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean. `cargo test`: 70/70 pass (incl. `write_tool_defaults_to_confirm`, `round_trips_assistant_tool_calls_and_tool_result`, `working_dir_round_trips_in_settings`).

## Findings

### Bugs (block ship)

1. **User can send while approval is parked** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs:790` (`send_message`) gates only on `self.streaming`, not `self.pending_approval.is_some()`. While the approval card is visible, `streaming = false`. A user who types + Enter (or clicks Send) pushes a new user msg and calls `start_streaming`, while the parked `ToolCall` stays in `pending_approval`. The orphan call's `id` then dangles — clicking the still-visible card later pushes a `Role::Tool` reply into a fresh, unrelated turn (its tool_call_id won't match the model's most recent assistant message, which providers reject). Fix: add `&& self.pending_approval.is_none() && self.pending_tool_calls.is_empty()` to the early-return in `send_message` (and disable the Send button + show "Tool waiting for approval").

2. **Stale pending state across conversation switches** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs:285,381,403` (`new_conversation`, `load_conversation`, `delete_conversation`) clear edit/draft state but not `pending_approval`, `pending_tool_calls`, `tool_iterations`, or `auto_approved_tools`. Switching conversations mid-approval keeps the card visible; clicking Approve runs the tool and pushes a `tool_result` into the *new* conversation's `messages`, with a `tool_call_id` from the old conv's assistant. Fix: clear all four in both flows; `stop_streaming` should arguably also clear pending tool state.

3. **`auto_approved_tools` is per-session, not per-conversation** — confirmed bug as the user suspected. `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs:226` is a session-wide `HashSet<String>` that's never reset. Hover text on the button at `:1454` says "in this conversation". The `process_pending_tool_calls` lookup at `:1389` and the `ApproveAll` arm at `:1417` make no per-conv distinction. Fix: either move into a `HashMap<i64, HashSet<String>>` keyed by `current_conversation_id`, or clear in `new_conversation`/`load_conversation`.

### Things to fix before ship (smaller)

4. **`tool_iterations` not reset by `regenerate`/`edit_and_resend`** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs:985,1039`. Only `send_message:841` resets the counter. A user who regenerates after the model already burned 6 iterations gets only 2 more before the cap fires — silent and surprising. Add `self.tool_iterations = 0;` to both.

5. **Missing-id tool calls silently dropped** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/api.rs:478-481` filters out slots without an `id`. Reasonable for safety but it can leave the model dangling (model thinks it called X, never sees a result, keeps trying). Worth at least an `eprintln!` so it's diagnosable, or surface as an error event.

### Things to flag / accept

6. **Path traversal stance** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/tools.rs:158` (`resolve_path`). Your take is reasonable: write_file/run_shell are `safety=confirm`, so the user sees the path before it executes; read-only tools (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`) at `safety=auto` *can* read `../../etc/passwd` without prompting, but the LLM has to have asked — and the `working_dir` is a user-set per-conv knob. Document this ("tool calls inherit your file-system permissions, including for read-only auto tools") in the tool TOMLs or release notes.

7. **`run_shell` 30s timeout** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/tools.rs:151`. Wrong tradeoff for a power-user dev tool. `cargo build`, `cargo test`, `npm install` all easily exceed 30s. The 50KB stdout cap is fine; the wall clock isn't. Bump to 300s, or make it a setting in the TOML (`timeout_secs = 300`).

8. **Streaming accumulator** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/api.rs:448` correctly handles per-slot out-of-order arrival, multi-call turns (Vec grows), and piecewise arg concat. Good.

9. **Storage migrations** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/storage.rs:128-180`. `tool_calls`/`tool_call_id`/`working_dir` follow the same `add_column_if_missing` pattern; all nullable, no NOT-NULL surprises. Safe for 0.6.x→0.9.0 upgrades.

10. **`save_current` persists `tool_calls`** — confirmed at `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/storage.rs:336` (UPDATE) and `:371` (INSERT). Crash mid-execution preserves the call list.

11. **Safety regression test** — only checks the embedded TOMLs. If someone flipped `Safety::default()` to `Confirm`, no test catches it (all TOMLs have explicit `safety` lines). Minor; add `assert_eq!(Safety::default(), Safety::Auto)` for belt-and-braces.

12. **`render_approval_card`'s `&mut pretty.as_str()`** — `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs:1448`. Works (binds a temporary), but mildly cursed Rust. `&mut pretty.as_ref()` or a named `let mut s = pretty.as_str();` reads cleaner. Cosmetic.

## Verdict

**needs-fixes-before-shipping** — items 1, 2, 3 are real correctness bugs that produce broken assistant→tool message chains. They're all small (a few lines each in `app.rs`). Items 4, 5, 7 are followups I'd want before tagging 0.9.0 but wouldn't block a beta on.

content.subject: Build, clippy, review subagent, PR + 0.9.0 release
content.description: Standard delivery ritual.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase 5a: native tools (read/list/search/write/shell + TOML format + execution loop)

Adds function/tool calling so the model can read files, search code,
run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Tools
ship as TOML files in ~/.config/hchat/tools/, with five defaults
seeded from embedded files on first launch. The format supports
hardcoded Rust handlers (`handler = "builtin:..."`) for the defaults
and shell-template handlers (`handler = { shell = ["argv", ...] }`)
for community-pack-able user-defined tools.

Default pack:
- read_file (auto): contents up to 100KB, with offset/limit for slicing
- list_directory (auto): files + dirs with d/ or f/ prefix
- search_files (auto): regex over recursive tree, skips dotdirs/binary
- write_file (confirm): with append mode, creates parent dirs
- run_shell (confirm): sh -c, captures stdout/stderr/exit, 300s timeout

Safety + approval flow:
- safety = "auto" tools execute silently
- safety = "confirm" tools park in a pending_approval slot until the
  user clicks Approve / Approve all in this conv / Reject in the
  approval card rendered above the input
- "Approve all in this conv" populates a per-conversation allowlist
  that's cleared on conversation switch
- Send is gated while an approval is parked or queue is non-empty so
  the assistant<->tool message chain can't be orphaned

Execution loop:
- After Done with tool_calls, drain auto-safety calls, push tool
  results, re-fire start_streaming with iterations++. Park the first
  confirm-safety call if any.
- Hard cap at 8 tool-then-restream cycles per user turn (toast on cap).
- Counter resets on send_message, regenerate, edit_and_resend.
- Tool execution state cleared on conversation new/load/delete so a
  parked card from one conv can't resolve into another.

API:
- ChatRequest gains a `tools` array (OpenAI shape).
- Streaming Delta picks up tool_calls deltas; api.rs accumulates
  per-slot across chunks (id arrives once, name once, arguments
  piecewise) and emits a single ToolCalls event before Done.
- Missing-id slots are dropped with an eprintln so the diagnostic
  doesn't disappear into the void.

Storage: messages.tool_calls (JSON), messages.tool_call_id, and
conversations.working_dir columns ALTERed in via the same
add_column_if_missing path used in 0.8.0 — safe across upgrades.

Message: gains tool_calls (Option<Vec<ToolCall>>), tool_call_id, and
Role::Tool. Message::tool_result(call_id, body) constructor.

UI:
- Settings panel exposes per-conversation working_dir (defaults to ~).
- Tool calls render as compact collapsible 🔧 blocks under the
  assistant message.
- Tool results render as 📤 blocks with collapsed body.
- Approval card shows tool name + pretty-printed args + 3 buttons.

Tests: 70 passing (22 new). TOML parse + safety defaults regression
+ each builtin handler + shell substitution + path resolution +
storage round-trip with tool_calls + Role::Tool.

Review fixes applied before push:
- send_message blocked while pending_approval/queue non-empty
- clear_tool_execution_state on new/load/delete conversation
- auto_approved_tools cleared per-conversation (not session-wide)
- tool_iterations reset on regenerate + edit
- shell timeout 30s -> 300s for typical dev workflows
- missing-id tool_call slots logged on drop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
